brief: Episode packaged into a publish-ready summary with three clip moments: why tiny binaries outperform bloated stacks, how artifact contracts reduce handoff friction, and why GitHub can act as the control plane for lightweight products. Show notes and repurposing copy are ready for release.
compositionNote: This one episode fans out into multiple deliverables with tiny single-purpose binaries. You can stop at clips, keep going to packaging, or swap in bonfyre-narrate and bonfyre-distribute without changing the rest of the stack.

outputNotes.show notes: bonfyre-brief and bonfyre-render generate a publishable summary layer from the cleaned transcript. This is the fastest lightweight output for an episode.
outputNotes.clip list: bonfyre-clips marks reusable moments from the same episode artifact, so short-form outputs stay linked to the full transcript and summary.
outputNotes.article draft: The article draft is the repurposing surface: the same transcript can become prose without rebuilding the pipeline around a separate content system.
outputNotes.RSS-ready bundle: bonfyre-pack and bonfyre-emit package the episode for actual distribution. This is where the app stops being a summary demo and becomes a small publishing system.
